name: example-mcp-app-observability
description: Interactive SRE workflows for Elastic Observability — Kubernetes incident investigation, ML anomaly triage, blast-radius assessment, and alert-rule creation.
long_description: An MCP App server that brings agentic SRE workflows directly into Claude Desktop and other MCP clients. Purpose-built for investigating Kubernetes incidents against live OpenTelemetry data in Elastic: watch for ML anomalies, explain them with an inline severity gauge, roll up cluster health, assess node blast-radius, map service dependencies, and create Kibana threshold alerts.

tools.name: watch
tools.description: Transient watcher — blocks until an ML anomaly fires or an ES|QL metric condition is met. Universal: metric mode needs no specific backend.

tools.name: ml-anomalies
tools.description: Query ML anomaly detection records. Requires: Elastic ML jobs configured.

tools.name: apm-health-summary
tools.description: Cluster-level health rollup from APM service telemetry, with K8s and ML context when available. Requires: Elastic APM (K8s/ML optional).

tools.name: k8s-blast-radius
tools.description: Assess the impact of a Kubernetes node going offline. Requires: Kubernetes (kubeletstats). APM is optional — enriches output with downstream user-facing service impact.

tools.name: apm-service-dependencies
tools.description: Service dependency graph from APM telemetry. Requires: Elastic APM.

tools.name: create-alert-rule
tools.description: Create a persistent Kibana custom-threshold alerting rule. Universal: works on any metric field in any index pattern.

kibana_api_key.title: Kibana API Key (optional)
kibana_api_key.description: API key for Kibana authentication. Leave blank to reuse the Elasticsearch API key — in most Elastic Cloud deployments the same key authenticates against both endpoints.
